The guest list includes many of

YINGST-BANkSON WEDDING.


Miss Ada Yingst and Mr.. L. c.
Bankson were quietly married last
evening at the home of the brides
parents, Mr.. and Mrs. ]. r. Yingst
on First avenue. The service was
read by the Rev. S. Willis McFad.
den OF the Presbyterian church
Preceding the ceremony Miss Mabel
lnkster of Davenport, Wash, played
Mendelssohns wedding march.


The bride wore a tailored suit of
gray and carried white carnations
and was attended by Miss Nell Mus.
tain, who was gowned in a pale blue
chiffon and carried pink carnations


Mr.. and Mrs. Bankson will be at
home after April Is at 81 Liberty
avenue. Mrs. Bankson has many
friends in this city and Mr.. Bank
son Is well known as assistant pur.
chasing agent of the Inland Railway
company.


4 & &


The Young Woman's Christian as
ISOLATION will turn their rooms over
today to the members of the Won.
ans Christian Temperance union
the occasion being the lecture to be
given by Mrs. Lulu Loveland Shep.
erd of Salt Lake City.


The rooms will be decorated in
the national colors-red, white and
blue. A general invitation is ex
tended to all church and club
women.
